zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Moore於1503年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾波文格頓時，她的父親Sir Christopher More29歲，母親Lady Margaret Mugge15歲。她於1524年與Ephraim Gould在英國素福爾可素斯 艾爾哈姆 聖 彼得結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1546年6月28日在英國賀特福爾享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Moore于1503年出生在英国英格兰賀特福爾波文格頓时，她的父亲Sir Christopher More29岁，母亲Lady Margaret Mugge15岁。她于1524年与Ephraim Gould在英国素福爾可素斯 艾爾哈姆 聖 彼得结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1546年6月28日在英国賀特福爾享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: French: nickname for a dark-skinned man, from a diminutive of Old French more ‘Moor’ (see More ).

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Moore was born in 1503, in Bovingdon, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Christopher More, was 29 and her mother, Lady Margaret Mugge, was 15. She married Ephraim Gould in 1524, in South Elmham St Peter, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 28 June 1546, in Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Moore est née en 1503, à Bovingdon, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Christopher More, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Mugge, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Ephraim Gould en 1524, à South Elmham St Peter, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 juin 1546, à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 43 ans.

ko.South Elmham St Peter, Suffolk, England: 영국 수폴크 소우스 엘함 스트 피터
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Moore(이)가 1503년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 보빙던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Christopher More(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Mugge(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1524년 영국 수폴크 소우스 엘함 스트 피터에서 Ephraim Gould(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1546년 6월 28일 영국 허트퍼드실르에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.South Elmham St Peter, Suffolk, England: соут элмхам ст питир, суффолк, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Moore родилась в 1503 г. в местности бовингдон, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Christopher More было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Mugge, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ephraim Gould в 1524 г. в местности соут элмхам ст питир, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 28 июня 1546 г. в местности хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 43 лет.
